purpose: Normalize CERT-style insider threat logs into a single canonical event stream suitable for chaining, anomaly detection, and audit-grade reporting.

fields.name: event_id
fields.description: Deterministic unique event identifier (e.g., hash of source + row + timestamp + user).

fields.name: ts
fields.description: Event timestamp in ISO-8601 UTC when possible.

fields.name: ts_epoch
fields.description: Optional epoch seconds for fast windowing.

fields.name: source_system
fields.description: Origin system/log type (e.g., device, file, email, web, psychometric).

fields.name: source_file
fields.description: The raw input filename this event came from.

fields.name: source_row
fields.description: Row number in source file (if applicable).

fields.name: scenario_id
fields.description: Optional scenario/case identifier when running multiple scenario files.

fields.name: user
fields.description: Canonical user identifier (employee id / username).

fields.name: user_role
fields.description: Optional role or org label if present.

fields.name: host
fields.description: Canonical endpoint/host identifier if available.

fields.name: src_ip
fields.description: Source IP if available.

fields.name: dst_ip
fields.description: Destination IP if available.

fields.name: event_type
fields.description: High-level event category.

fields.name: action
fields.description: Canonical action name (e.g., file_open, file_copy, email_send, web_visit, logon, process_start).

fields.name: object_type
fields.description: Type of primary object acted upon.

fields.name: object_name
fields.description: Human-readable object identifier (filename, url, process name).

fields.name: file_path
fields.description: Full path for file-related events when available.

fields.name: file_ext
fields.description: Lowercased extension (e.g., doc, pdf, zip).

fields.name: file_size_bytes
fields.description: File size in bytes if available.

fields.name: email_from
fields.description: Sender address/user if available.

fields.name: email_to
fields.description: Primary recipient or recipient list (stringified) if available.

fields.name: email_cc
fields.description: CC recipients (stringified) if available.

fields.name: email_subject
fields.description: Email subject if available.

fields.name: has_attachments
fields.description: True if email/send event includes attachments.

fields.name: attachment_count
fields.description: Number of attachments on the email/send event.

fields.name: attachment_total_bytes
fields.description: Sum of attachment sizes in bytes.

fields.name: attachment_types
fields.description: Comma-separated list of attachment extensions/types (e.g., pdf,doc,zip).

fields.name: url
fields.description: URL for web events if available.

fields.name: domain
fields.description: Parsed domain for web events if available.

fields.name: process_name
fields.description: Process/binary name if applicable.

fields.name: command_line
fields.description: Command line if applicable.

fields.name: outcome
fields.description: Outcome if provided by source.

fields.name: tags
fields.description: Comma-separated analyst/system tags (optional).

fields.name: raw
fields.description: Optional raw record payload (stringified) for provenance/debug.

notes: This schema is designed to tolerate partial sources; normalizers should populate required_minimum and fill the rest opportunistically.
Downstream chaining (exfil logic) relies primarily on user, ts, event_type/action, file_ext, attachment_count/bytes.
